title: The last morning
body:
Woke up to frost on the van windows and Wojo curled into my sleeping bag like a furry comma. This is the last morning in Bend — the town where we planned all of this, spread maps across the floor of a brewery, and decided to just go.

The van is packed tight. Every cabinet has a purpose. Wojo has claimed the passenger seat as his throne and the dash as his lookout. I think he knows something is different.

We're heading north first, up through the gorge. No real reason except the river pulls me that way. Then east. Then south. Then wherever the road feels right.

Goodbye, Bend. Thanks for the launchpad.

title: Wind and water
body:
The Columbia Gorge is a wind tunnel with opinions. Parked at a pullout near Beacon Rock and the van rocked all night like a boat. Wojo was not amused.

Spent the morning watching kiteboarders tear across the river — tiny bright sails against all that gray water and green cliff. The scale of this place is hard to photograph. Everything is vertical.

Found a little cafe in Stevenson that let Wojo sit on the patio. He watched a dog three times his size with the calm superiority only a cat can manage. The barista brought him a saucer of cream without asking.

We'll head east tomorrow. Idaho by nightfall if the passes are clear.
